title: Maestro What-If (Feasibility Study)
description: Single source of truth for one /what-if feasibility study. A what-if drives ONLY the front half of the pipeline (strategist + principal engineer) then STOPS — it never enters implementation or verification, writes no source, produces no diff. It terminates in the what-if-specific terminal state WHATIF_DONE carrying a feasibility verdict, or bounces to NEEDS_HUMAN (a requirement bounce, NOT a verdict). Persisted under `.maestro/whatifs/<id>/`, deliberately separate from `.maestro/tasks/` so a study is never mistaken for a built/verified task. The orchestrator is the ONLY writer. Self-contained: history and token_usage shapes are copied from task-status (not $ref'd across files).

whatif_id.description: Stable what-if id, e.g. WHATIF-001. The WHATIF- prefix is one of three redundant signals that this is a study, not a built task.

question.description: The raw, verbatim free-text question the human asked. Never overwritten — the immutable north star for the strategist and principal engineer.

stage.description: Where the study currently sits. Drives routing. Deliberately EXCLUDES IMPLEMENTATION/VERIFICATION/etc — a what-if can never reach them. WHATIF_DONE is the success terminal (carries a verdict); NEEDS_HUMAN is a strategist bounce (no verdict).

refs.description: Pointers (relative file paths under the what-if folder) to the durable artifacts each stage produces.

requirement_ref.description: Strategist's feasibility-framed requirement + acceptance criteria doc.

architecture_ref.description: Principal engineer's feasibility study / approach-if-buildable doc.

context_requirements.description: Per-agent whitelist of which `refs` fields the orchestrator may assemble into that agent's prompt. Only strategist and principal-engineer ever participate in a what-if.

feasibility_verdict.description: The synthesized verdict, mirrored here from verdict.json once ARCHITECTURE completes. Null until WHATIF_DONE. Same shape as the standalone verdict.json root.

history.description: Append-only audit trail. Every routing decision, verdict, and normalization the orchestrator makes is logged here — including BOTH the raw agent output reference and the normalized record — so a human can reconstruct exactly what each agent saw and why.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601. Supplied by the orchestrator from a shell `date` call, since agents cannot read the clock.

raw_ref.description: Path to the raw agent output captured for this step, when applicable.

token_usage.description: Best-effort token accounting; sole source is the <usage>subagent_tokens: N</usage> scalar the orchestrator sees per Agent return. Observability only — NEVER read by routing or verdict synthesis. Missing/malformed usage is a null-token turn, never an error.

verdict.description: A synthesized feasibility verdict. Also the root shape of the standalone verdict.json artifact. Honest by construction: an accurate INFEASIBLE is a valid, valuable outcome; token_usage never influences it.

reasoning.description: Why this verdict — grounded in the strategist's scope and the principal engineer's plan/risks.

evidence_refs.description: Concrete file/artifact citations supporting the verdict. At least one is always required.

blocking_constraint.description: The specific constraint that conditions (CAVEATS) or prevents (INFEASIBLE) the build. FEASIBLE may leave this null; CAVEATS and INFEASIBLE must name it (non-empty string).

promotable.description: True iff verdict != INFEASIBLE. FEASIBLE and FEASIBLE_WITH_CAVEATS are promotable via `/maestro run <whatif_id>`; INFEASIBLE is refused.
